title: Compliance Officer EMEIA
description:
Can you bring your legal and compliance experience to our team? Join our teams in Czech Republic, Poland, Finland, UK, Germany or Sweden and enjoy a collaborative culture that empowers you to build a career you can be proud of.
   

  

**What you would do as our** 
**Compliance Officer EMEIA**
 The Divisional Compliance Officer will report to the ASSA ABLOY Opening Solutions Vice-President & General Counsel EMEIA and shall oversee compliance matters across the division. The successful candidate can be based from one of our sites across Europe.
   

  

 You would also:
   

  

* Act as a key member of the Legal & Compliance function, supporting in the development, implementation and ongoing oversight of the Group’s compliance programs including anti-corruption, anti-trust and export control/trade sanctions and help identify, manage and solve compliance risks, and advise on compliance topics.
* Design, develop, and continuously improve the EMEIA Division’s compliance programs and keep abreast of relevant regulatory developments across the EMEIA Division, raise awareness and adoption of compliance programs through regular compliance training.
* Continuously follow up on the processes, procedures and implementation of compliance policies and report the results to relevant stakeholders.
* Work with the M&A Team and external advisors for compliance due diligence and post-acquisition integration.
* Conduct compliance investigations and anti-corruption reviews in conjunction with Internal Audit and external consultants, regularly reporting on such matters to the Group’s Code of Conduct Committee and professionally manage communications with regulators as may be deemed necessary.
* and any other duties required to assist in supporting the achievement of Group objectives.


**The Skills And Experience You Need**
 We are looking for someone who:
   

  

* Is fluent in English both verbal and written.
* Is educated to at least a degree level in a relevant subject, preferably legal qualification and has minimum of 5 years of proven experience in a similar position, prior experience in internal audit or working with internal controls is an advantage.
* Has experience working in the Manufacturing or Engineering industry in an international environment (would be a distinct advantage) and is proficient in MS Office 365, particularly Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Outlook and Teams.
* Has excellent communication skills with the ability to influence and build trust at all levels of the business and ability to work under pressure, make decisions and produce results with limited supervision.
* Is a self-starter with an ability to manage multiple priorities and I prepared to travel within the region and occasionally outside of it.


**What We Offer**
 We’re passionate about providing amazing opportunities and benefits, so you can continue and progress a lifelong career with us – here’s what we have to offer:
   

  

* Learning and career development opportunities, whether it’s online learning, management training or enhancing your skills
* A competitive salary and incentive schemes
* A dynamic working environment with a good team spirit where personal development and growth are highly encouraged.


**We review applications regularly, so don’t wait**
 We are building diverse, inclusive teams, and encourage applications from everyone who can see themselves working with us. Just set up your profile and apply here.
   

  

 To make sure your personal data is safe, we don’t look at any applications sent by email or post. If you have any questions about the role or the process, email Monika Fikrová, Talent Acquisition Business Partner, at monika.fikrova@assaabloy.com .
   

  

 Let’s create a safer and more open world - together!
   

  

 To find out more about us, visit www.assaabloy.com
   

  

**We are the ASSA ABLOY Group**
 Our people have made us the global leader in access solutions. In return, we open doors for them wherever they go. With nearly 63,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries, we help billions of people experience a more open world. Our innovations make all sorts of spaces – physical and virtual – safer, more secure, and easier to access.
   

  

 As an employer, we value results – not titles, or backgrounds. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally.
   

  

 As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

title: Agentic AI Co-Founder / CMO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.
   

  

 We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.
   

  

 Our offer:
   

  

* A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.
* 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis
* Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors
* Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)
* Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine
* Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)


 One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.
   

  

**Tasks**
* You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Agentic AI
* You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management
* You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs
* You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding
* You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively


**Requirements**
* You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate
* You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues
* You have excellent communication skills in the English language


 Join us and build a €1B+ company with us!

title: Forward Deployed AI Scientist, Sweden - BCG X
description:
Who We Are
   

  

 Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.
   

  

 To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.
   

  

**We Are BCG X**
 We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
   

  

 Our Data Scientists and Senior Data Scientist are part of our rapidly growing team to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.
   

  

 Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, creative, and communicative.
   

  

 We Are Looking For Dedicated Individuals With a Passion For Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research And Redefining Organizations Into AI Led Innovative Companies. Successful Candidates Possess The Following
   

  

* Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
* Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
* Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
* Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
* Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
* Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
* Have strong project management skills


 What You'll Bring
   

  

**Technologies**
 Programming Languages: Python
   

  

 #BCGXjob
   

  

 Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
   

  

 BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

title: SAP Super Key User
description:
Do you want to work in a global company where you can contribute to a more sustainable food production? DeLaval is a leading developer, manufacturer, and supplier of solutions for dairy farmers around the world. As a company built on innovation, we constantly work to find ways of helping our customers do more with less.
   

  

**Role And Responsibilities**
 As an SAP Super User, you will drive ERP process development and digitalization in final assembly operations. You’ll be a key player in SAP utilization, standardization, and digital transformation at the Tumba factory, aligned with global supply chain initiatives.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* First-line support for local Key Users
* Ensure ERP and digital projects are delivered on time and within budget
* Document processes and functional specifications
* Lead SAP training and continuous process improvements
* Drive Industry 4.0 initiatives (e.g., CPS, RFID, AGV, energy monitoring)
* Support smart factory roadmap and CO₂ reduction goals


**Qualifications**
 To be successful in this role we believe that you have:
   

  

* Min. Bachelor’s Degree (Mechanical engineering, data communication or equivalent educational background)
* Min. 3 years of experience in operations (production and/or warehousing)
* Experience in usage of Lean principles (flow based on demand, standardized work, perfect quality)
* Understanding the general Industry 4.0 applications (CPS, RFID, NFC, Augmented Reality, Autonomous Guided Vehicle, interfaces and standards etc.)
* Sense of ownership even under high pressure situations
* Computer knowledge in SAP, Office 365 etc
* Project management practical skills
* Swedish & English language skills (ability to communicate and lead trainings in local language & English)


**Working at DeLaval**
 Our core values Passion, People, Professionals and Partnerships connect individuals across entities and geographies. Success is far more than knowledge – it is strongly related to behaviour. At DeLaval we are all guided by our three behaviours: Set Direction, Make Things Happen and One DeLaval.
   

  

 We Offer You a Workplace Like No Other, Where State-of-the-art Technology Goes Hand In Hand With Animal Welfare. DeLaval Is a Part Of The Tetra Laval Group Together With Tetra Pak And Sidel. In Addition, We Offer
   

  

* Secure employment and benefits from the collective agreement (occupational pension, work-related accident insurance).
* Parental leave compensation (approx. 10 percent of the employee's monthly salary).
* Wellness allowance of SEK 4 000/year.
* Private healthcare insurance.
* Flexible working hours and reduced working hours.
* 30 days paid annual leave.


**Additional Information**
 This is a full-time permanent position based at DeLaval’s headquarters in Tumba, south of Stockholm. We offer a hybrid workplace model, allowing flexibility to work remotely.
   

  

 We review applications ongoing. We do not accept applications via email, however if you have any questions regarding the role or the recruitment process don’t hesitate to contact us. Please note that response times may be longer than usual due to holiday periods. We appreciate your patience and understanding.
   

  

**Contact**
 Hiring manager: Gustav Nordlander, gustav.nordlander@delaval.com
   

  

 Recruiter: Agnes Edbom, agnes.edbom@delaval.com
   

  

**About DeLaval**
 Our vision is to make sustainable food production possible. As a company built on innovation, we constantly work to find ways of helping our customers, dairy farmers, do more with less by providing world-leading milking equipment and solutions.
   

  

 Today, DeLaval has approximately 4,500 passionate professionals operating in more than 100 markets around the world. DeLaval is part of the Tetra Laval Group.
   

  

 See more at corporate.delaval.com

title: Sales Director – Own the New Business Engine at ACEVE
description:
The construction industry is one of the last major sectors yet to be fully transformed by technology. At ACEVE, we're changing that — and we're looking for a Sales Director to help lead the charge.
   

  

 The Opportunity
   

  

 We are a Nordic SaaS company with a clear mission: to become Europe's leading platform for construction and skilled trades. Market-leading in Sweden, EQT-backed, and evolving fast as an AI-first company — we are at an inflection point where the right commercial leadership will define what the next chapter looks like.
   

  

 This is a key role for ACEVE. As Sales Director, you will own new business growth in Sweden, driving the commercial agenda from end to end — from how we generate and qualify leads, to how we go to market, to how we close and land customers for long-term success. You will shape the strategy and live in the execution, with the authority and the platform to make a real difference.
   

  

 This is not a role for someone looking to maintain the status quo. It's a role for a leader who has built high-performing SaaS sales organisations before, knows what great looks like, and is energised by the opportunity to do it again — at scale, with momentum behind them.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 Reporting to our Swedish Managing Director, you will lead sales managers across Net New Sales, SDR, Solution Specialists, and Specialty Sales — bringing the organisation together around one clear commercial direction.
   

  

**Your Key Responsibilities**
* Own ACEVE's new business commercial strategy — including segmentation, GTM execution, pipeline prioritisation, and conversion from qualified lead to closed-won
* Build the structure, playbooks, and processes that allow the sales organisation to perform consistently and win at scale
* Coach and develop your sales managers, setting a high bar for performance, accountability, and commercial rigour
* Drive daily execution — pipeline reviews, deal coaching, forecast accuracy — while keeping the team focused on outcomes, not activity
* Partner with Marketing on positioning and messaging that opens the right doors
* Collaborate with Customer Success and Product to ensure new customers land well and are set up for long-term success
* Contribute to Sales Leadership strategy and help shape how ACEVE sells across the organisation


 Who You Are
   

  

 You're a senior SaaS sales leader who has done this before. You've built and scaled commercial teams, driven new business growth in competitive markets, and created cultures where people are developed, held accountable, and proud of what they deliver.
   

  

 You understand the full commercial picture — from demand generation and GTM strategy to daily coaching and CRM discipline — and you know how to connect the dots across all of it. You lead with clarity, act with urgency, and bring out the best in the people around you.
   

  

**We're Looking For Someone Who Brings**
* 10+ years in sales within the software industry, including significant experience leading sales organisations focused on new business growth
* A proven track record of building and developing high-performing teams — with strong coaching instincts and high standards
* Deep familiarity with the full commercial engine: lead generation, GTM strategy, pipeline management, win rates, and ARR growth
* The ability to create clarity from complexity — in strategy, process, and expectations
* Experience reading and acting on commercial metrics with confidence and precision
* Confidence operating in a scaling environment where things move fast and leadership matters


 Why Join ACEVE
   

  

 We back the builders of society — the craftsmen and construction professionals who shape and maintain the world around us. Our platform gives them more time, more clarity, and more confidence in their everyday work. It's a mission worth getting behind.
   

  

 We are market-leading in Sweden — and that's not where we stop. Backed by EQT and driven by the ambition to become Europe's leading platform for construction and skilled trades, we are building for what comes next. As an AI-first company, we are evolving fast — in how we build, how we work, and how we grow.
   

  

 You'll join a leadership team that values initiative, a company that backs its people, and a role where your decisions will directly shape what comes next.
   

  

 If you've built great sales organisations before and you're ready to do it again — in a company that's going somewhere — we'd love to hear from you.
   

  

 Aceve is in business to back the builders of society. With over 900 employees, 100 million € in revenue and extensive experience in the field, we combine tradition and tech to provide a pioneering platform that helps our customers to achieve more.

title: Automationsingenjör SCADA
description:
**Nu vill vi stärka teamet med fler erfarna SCADA-programmerare (Plant Scada)**
 Tillsammans formar vi på Init Sweden framtiden för industriell IT och automation. Vi har en ambition att samla de bästa företagen och människorna inom industriell IT och Automation med en total omsättning på 1,5 mrd. SEK och snart 1000 anställda i Danmark, Sverige, Norge, Serbien och Spanien mfl., som är specialister inom industriell IT, automation, IOT och OT cybersäkerhet
   

  

 Vi har en spännande resa nu med ett jättefint tillskott av nya kollegor som valt att dela den med oss. Till detta så skall vi stärka våra relation med befintliga kunder och leta nya affärer där INIT skall vara det självklara valet inom Infrastruktur, Industri samt Fastighet. Ifjol växte vi med ett kontor i Kista och nu i starten av 2026 gick flytten av vårt andra kontor till större i Liljeholmen”
   

  

* Roger Löfgren, Business Unit Manager


**Om jobbet**
 Här ges du bl.a. möjlighet att ta plats i vår nya projektgrupp och med oss fortsätta utveckla förtroendet i vårt nyvunna ramavtal inom infrastruktur gällande samtliga tunnlar i Stockholm.
   

  

 Som senior SCADA-programmerare kommer du att arbeta i ett eller flera projekt tillsammans med andra kollegor där du är med och utvecklar dina kunder. Våra projekt utvecklas på vårt kontor och vi levererar till branschens största aktörer. Vi är en fabrikatsoberoende leverantör med etablerade partnersamarbeten med de största produktleverantörerna på marknaden. Det innebär att du hos oss får möjlighet att förkovra dig inom ett eller flera olika system.
   

  

 Du och teamet ansvarar tillsammans för att vi levererar det vi lovat i rätt tid, med rätt kvalitetskrav till rätt budget. Storleken på uppdragen kan variera.
   

  

**Arbetsuppgifter**
* Nyutveckling, utveckling, underhåll och daglig drift av kundernas system.
* Arbete kopplat till att sätta upp databaser samt analysera data.
* Visst arbete kopplat till virtualisering och konfiguration av nätverk
* Identifiering av förbättringsmöjligheter


**Om dig**
 Du har gedigen erfarenhet inom industriell automation och kombinerar teknisk kompetens med goda, upparbetade kundrelationer via din kommunikationsförmåga. Du är nyfiken, självgående och trivs i en roll där du får ta tekniskt ansvar samtidigt som du har kontakt med kunder. Med en prestigelös och flexibel inställning bidrar du till vår positiva arbetsmiljö.
   

  

 Önskelistan vad gäller erfarenhet och kvalifikationer skulle kunna beskrivas enligt följande:
   

  

* En eftergymnasial utbildning inom automation, systemvetenskap eller motsvarande erfarenhet som kan bedömas som likvärdig.
* Erfarenhet av att jobba med automation inom infrastruktur, inom industri och/eller fastigheter
* Har minst fem års arbetslivserfarenhet av rollen som automationsingenjör med SCADA-programmering, t.ex. av Citect eller Webport
* Du talar och skriver svenska obehindrat
* Svenskt medborgarskap är ett krav beroende på att flera av uppdragen hos våra kunder omfattas av säkerhetsskyddslagen.


**Meriterande Är**
* Certifiering inom Citect Scada
* Ett brett kontaktnät där du driver dina egna kunder


**What’s In It For Me**
 På Init får du det bästa av två världar: Du får ta del av de fördelar och möjligheter som kommer med att vara en del av ett större företag – tillgång till ledande projekt, ett brett nätverk och stora utvecklingsmöjligheter –samtidigt som du får uppleva entreprenörsanda, gemenskapen och den personliga känslan i ett mindre team där vi verkligen bryr oss om varandra.
   

  

 Tillsammans är vi Init – både i hur vi formar vår arbetsplats och i hur vi samarbetar med kunder för att forma framtidens bransch
   

  

**✔️** 
 Friskvårdsbidrag
   

  

**✔️** 
 27 dagars semester
   

  

 ✔️Tjänstepension(ITP)
   

  

 ✔️Föräldraledighetstillägg
   

  

 ✔️Förmånscykel
   

  

 ✔️48h/år kompetensutveckling
   

  

 ✔️Årlig (frivillig) hälsoundersökning och sjukvårdsförsäkring
   

  

 ✔️Flex och hybrid
   

  

**Placeringsort:** 
 Stockholm Liljeholmen eller Kista
   

  

 Vi läser ansökningar och träffar kandidater löpande så skicka in din ansökan redan idag! Vid frågor, kontakta mig, Rekryterare Sara Rensfeldt Romin, på sara.rensfeldt@initgroup.com
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! #Init2winit
   

  

 Init hjälper verksamheter att göra styrning, övervakning och systemintegration smartare. Vi kombinerar affär, organisation och teknik så att allt hänger ihop i praktiken.
   

  

 Vi är vana vid miljöer med höga krav på effektivitet, tillgänglighet och säkerhet. Därför tar vi ansvar hela vägen: från idé och design till programmering, driftsättning och löpande underhåll. Då kan kunden fokusera på sin kärnverksamhet.
   

  

 Vi har gjort det här i mer än 30 år, i fastigheter, industri och infrastruktur – alltid med samma mål: att göra komplexa system enklare att använda.

title: Senior Offshore/Onshore Pipeline Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
**Offshore/Onshore Senior Pipeline Engineer**
 Do you want to be at the heart of the energy transition industry, guiding the public and private sector in the delivery of technologies and systems to meet global and national net zero targets? Are you looking to bring your expertise and enthusiasm into play in developing a global, high performing design group delivering a range of innovative energy transition projects? Do you want to play a lead role in growing and nurturing a team in an open, collaborative, and empowering culture?
   

  

 If this sounds like you, or you’re curious to learn more, then this role could be the perfect opportunity. Join our Pipelines team and work with us to close the gap to a sustainable future.
   

  

**Your new role**
 As our new colleague, you'll join a dynamic and high performing team overseeing diverse global design projects, ranging from traditional gas infrastructure to green initiatives with a growing portfolio of offshore and onshore hydrogen and CO2 pipelines as well as cables for offshore windfarms. As we are busy on multiple and diversified projects, we have good upcoming opportunities, and your role and career path in our team will be tailored to your profile and professional ambitions. In your role you will contribute with:
   

  

  

* Providing engineering expertise to global multi-disciplinary projects by developing engineering studies, numerical analyses and technical reports, input to drawings, specifications and design philosophies.
* Contributing to /leading and/or managing our projects if you have experience with or a flair for that
* Supporting our Clients in delivering a growing portfolio of studies ranging from feasibility studies, FEED, detailed design through to construction, commissioning, and handover
* Working closely with many colleagues across disciplines ensuring that quality and deadlines are met
* Supporting tenders and business development activities
* Growing into our fantastic team benefitting from both a local an international environment


**About You**
* Master’s degree in mechanical, structural or a related field
* Proven experience with offshore and or onshore pipeline engineering and related standards. Experience within offshore cable engineering can also be of relevance.
* Experience in leading /technical management roles, or a flair for it will be highly evaluated
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
* Proactiveness and ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
* Willingness to grow with us and to welcome new challenges
* Strong communication and presentation skills
* Strong oral and written skills in English


**What We Can Offer You**
* Commitment to your development
* Leaders guided by our Leadership Principles
* A culture that welcomes you as the unique person you are
* Inspiration from colleagues, clients, and projects
* The long-term thinking of a foundation-owned company


**Ready to join us?**
 Please submit your application and CV online. We invite diversity in all its forms and encourage applicants from all groups to apply.
   

  

 Deadline: 22/05/2026
   

  

**Work at the heart of sustainable change with Ramboll**
 Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. As a foundation-owned people company, founded in Denmark, we believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. Our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act and being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. Ramboll employs more than 18,000 people globally across 35 countries. Ramboll experts deliver innovative solutions across Buildings, Transport, Water, Environment & Health, Energy, Management Consulting, and Architecture & Landscape. Combining local experience with global knowledge, we help shape the societies of tomorrow.
   

  

**Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion**
 Equality, diversity, and inclusion are at the heart of what we do. At Ramboll, we believe that diversity is a strength and that different experiences and perspectives are essential to creating truly sustainable societies. We are committed to providing an inclusive and supportive work environment where everyone is able to flourish and reach their potential. We also know how important it is to achieve the right balance of where, when, and how much you work. At Ramboll, we offer flexibility as part of our positive and inclusive approach to work. We invite applications from candidates of all backgrounds and characteristics. Please let us know if there are any changes we could make to the application process to make it more comfortable for you. You can contact us at job.advert.accessibility@ramboll.com with such requests.

title: Senior Manager: Consumer Strategy, Insights & Activation
description:
Are you passionate about building true consumer preference—turning deep insights and clear strategies into coordinated cross-functional execution and measurable user impact?
   

  

 We are looking for a business-driven leader who balances strategic vision with analytical depth and a pragmatic, execution-focusedmindset. You will play a fundamental role in enabling, guiding, and coordinating our company-wide path to becoming the undisputed #1 service in the eyes of our consumers.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 As a senior leader within the Consumer department, you will collaborate closely with the VP Consumer and our cross-functional team of product managers, designers, analysts, business developers and project managers. Your mission is to develop a small high-performing team of consumer analysts and project managers and to build the strategic foundation, insights and governance required to ensure the entire organization is aligned, informed, and accountable for delivering a superior consumer experience across functions and markets.
   

  

* Build the Strategic & Insights Foundation
* Define the Consumer North Star: You will drive the development and evolution of our Consumer Preference & Loyalty Strategy—defining who we target, what makes us the superior choice, and how we turn one-time users into loyal consumers.
* Own the Consumer Scorecard: You will define and manage our consumer KPIs (NPS, Brand Preference, User Ratings, CSAT, Active users etc.), ensuring a single, trusted source of truth for how we are performing in the eyes of our consumers.
* Lead Consumer Analytics: You will lead and empower our expert consumer analysts to establish a solid foundation for consumer, brand, loyalty and experience data and analytics and to make consumer insights accessible and actionable across the company.
* Operationalize the Consumer Agenda
* Establish Cross-Functional Governance: You will help design forums and governance structures that keep Group functions and Markets aligned—ensuring consumer impact is clearly integrated into strategies, priorities, and execution across the business.
* Drive Prioritized Strategic Initiatives: In collaboration with dedicated Project Manager(s), you will oversee some high-priority, cross-functional projects, ensuring these initiatives are executed effectively and tied to measurable consumer impact.
* Provide Transparency on Impact: You will establish shared frameworks to help the business follow up on execution and impact on our consumer KPIs.
* Strategic Consumer Champion & End-2-End Consumer Perspective
* Strategic Sparring: You will partner with the VP Consumer and other senior colleagues to provide consumer expertise across the organization, and shape structured ways of working to scale and accelerate cross-functional consumer excellence and execution.
* Champion the End-to-End Experience: While functional teams drive excellence within their respective domains, your team provides the "horizontal" lens and the concrete guidance required to ensure alignment and consistency across the full consumer journey and lifetime. Thereby ensuring that strategy, brand, product development, communication, service and operations are turned into coherent consumer experiences.


 Your Profile
   

  

* Senior Consumer Background: You bring 8–10+ years of experience from senior consumer-focused roles within a fast-paced service or tech environment. You have solid experience developing consumer strategies, translating them into concrete product, service and experience guidance, and delivering real business and user impact.
* Strong Analytical Maturity: You have solid experience working with consumer insights and data-driven strategy. You understand the drivers behind consumer behavior and know how to turn insights into clear priorities and actionable direction for the business.
* Solid CX, Product, Loyalty Expertise: You have strong strategic and hands-on experience from consumer-centric product development, CX, service innovation, loyalty, CRM or similar. You are comfortable moving between high level strategic frameworks and the operational details that make it work in practice.
* End-to-End & Cross-Functional Mindset: You are a systems thinker who sees the full consumer journey across touchpoints and functions. You have credibility with experienced Strategy, Product, Design and Operations teams and you are comfortable influencing without formal ownership.
* Leadership & Facilitation Skills: You are a trusted leader with a proven track record of coaching and developing high-performing talent. You are experienced in building alignment with leaders and experts across the organization, and a skilled facilitator of complex cross-functional work and forums.
* Strategic but Pragmatic: You think big picture and long term, but you aren't afraid to get into the details of cross-functional execution. You care about measurable impact and understand the realities of operating in a complex, service-driven organization.
* Education: You have a Master’s degree in Business, Engineering, or other relevant field.


 What You'll Get
   

  

 We can promise you that you will be challenged, develop your skills and have the opportunity to work with truly amazing and competent colleagues. If you like changes and want to set new processes and ways of working - this is the place for you!
   

  

**Benefits**
 Of course, we have all the necessary and standard benefits in place, but in addition to this, we can also offer:
   

  

* Sky-High Office: Modern and newly renovated with a 360-degree view around Stockholm.
* Puppy Friendly: Bring your dog to work - we've got a whole floor for our furry friends!
* Fun & Games: Ping pong, Shuffleboard, Foosball, and even a Karaoke Room for your leisure time.
* Flexible Hours: Hybrid setup with working hours that adapt to your lifestyle.
* Parental Pay for Six Months: Step away from work and provide the best care and love to your little one.
* A Day Off on You Birthday: We love to celebrate, so enjoy your birthday to the fullest with a day off each year!


**Get To Know Us**
 In 2022, Instabee was founded after combining Budbee and Instabox - two leading last-mile delivery companies with three consumer-facing brands: Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy. Instabee serves millions of consumers across Europe with convenient deliveries and is on track to become the leading European e-commerce enabler. Always with our people, the merchants and our consumers at the heart of everything we do.
   

  

 We’re fast, both in terms of parcel delivery and when it comes to growing our business. So far our journey has taken us from Sweden to the rest of Scandinavia and parts of continental Europe, but we have no plans on stopping anytime soon. As we continue to expand, the complexity of Instabee grows in parallel. With that in mind, we’re now looking for you to help us on the journey ahead.
   

  

*This recruitment process consists of background checks via Verifiera as a review of previous public criminal records. The result is public information that is handled confidentially. This is mandatory for all recruitment processes at Instabee (Instabox/Budbee) in Sweden.*
 Instabee Group, with consumer-facing brands Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy, brings together years of experience in delivery. Today, Instabee Group is present in five markets, serving thousands of online merchants and millions of consumers with seamless home deliveries and convenient parcel locker deliveries, using the best fuel available. With smart technology, a strong focus on sustainability, and a secure supply chain, Instabee has become a trusted partner for online merchants and a preferred choice for consumers.

title: H&M - Junior Fashion Designer - Regional Assortment
description:
**Job Description**

 Are you a creatively driven
 **Junior Designer** 
 with a strong fashion instinct and a sharp commercial eye?
   

  





 We are looking for an emerging talent to join our Regional Assortment team—someone ready to shape globally relevant collections through a distinctly local perspective. Based at our Stockholm Head Office, this role focuses on our Equatorial markets, translating global vision into culturally nuanced, fashion-forward design with real commercial impact.
   

  





 As a Junior Designer within Regional Relevance, you will work closely with Senior Designers to build curated, market-specific collections that speak directly to the customer while remaining aligned with the overarching brand direction. You will contribute to defining a modern, relevant assortment that balances creativity with wearability across diverse regions.
   

  





 You will be immersed in a highly collaborative environment, working alongside a cross-functional team including Product Managers, Pattern Makers, Print Designers, Planners, Buyers, Product Developers, and Business Controllers. This role offers hands-on exposure across the full design process—from sketching and evolving collection overviews to supporting technical packs and contributing to concept development.
   

  





**WHO YOU ARE**




 We are looking for people with:
   

  




* A BA degree in Textile/Fashion Design
* At least 1 year’s work experience as a Junior Designer
* Passion and understanding of our H&M customer
* Strong skills in Adobe Suite programs
* Experience in 3D CLO is a plus
* Attention to detail and fabric knowledge
* A creative and visionary mindset for fashion
* Strong communication skills and flexibility
* Ability to prioritize with an organized approach
* Enjoy working in diverse teams and contributing your perspective
* Thrive in a fast-paced, ever-changing environment
* Strong self-drive and initiative
* English fluency (Swedish is not necessary)



**WHO WE ARE​**




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​
   

  





 ​We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
   

  





 ​
 **WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE:**
  

 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​
   

  





 All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET, Afound. In addition to our staff discount, all our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program – HIP. You can read more about our H&M Incentive Program here
 




**JOIN US​**




 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​
   

  





 ​Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​
   

  





 If you would like to be considered for our Open Application, please apply by sending in your
 **CV and portfolio** 
 pdf (max 10mb/upload). All documents should be in English.
   

  





***​Please note:***
 For any internal applications you must speak to your current Manager first before applying.
   

  





 The journey starts here

title: H&M - Swimwear Designer - Womenswear
description:
**Job Description**

 Are you an experienced Designer with an instinct for sun-drenched aesthetics, elevated minimalism, and bold femininity? We are looking for a Swimwear Designer to create collections that capture the energy of the season—effortless, confident, and globally relevant.
   

  





 As a Swimwear Designer for H&M you will shape and define collections that move seamlessly from beach to city, where swim becomes ready-to-wear. You will translate trend direction into refined, commercial product with a strong fashion perspective.
   

  





 From sculpted silhouettes to standout prints and considered detailing, you will design with precision and intention, balancing creativity with wearability.
   

  





 You will own the design process from initial concept through to final product, building cohesive assortments that resonate across diverse markets while maintaining a clear and elevated aesthetic.
   

  





**More about the role....**
  

  

  

 You’ll be part of a dynamic, cross-functional team shaping collections for a global stage delivering fashion-forward product to diverse markets through H&M Group omni channels.
   

  





 We’re looking for a Designer with a distinct creative handwriting, someone who lives and breathes swimwear, with an instinctive feel for trend, silhouette, and cultural relevance. You bring a strong fashion perspective, effortlessly balancing elevated design with commercial impact.
   

  





 In this role, you’ll move at pace, fuelled by creativity, curiosity, and a constant drive to evolve. You’ll collaborate, influence, and inspire those around you, using your experience to guide and elevate the team while delivering product that resonates on a global scale.
   

  





 A sharp eye, a fast mind, and a passion for turning ideas into covetable pieces will set you apart.
   

  

  

  





**WHO YOU ARE** 
  

  





 We are looking for a Designer with:
   

  




* BA/MA in Fashion/Textile Design or equivalent experience
* 3-4 years’ minimum experience as a Designer, with strength in collection building
* Experience with global brands or retailers
* Strong fabric knowledge and attention to detail
* Passion for women’s fashion and trends
* Customer-focused and commercial mindset
* Experience with suppliers/factories in Asia and Europe
* Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite programs while 3D CLO is meriting
* Strong communication and teamwork skills
* Leadership and coaching experience
* Independent, proactive, and organized
* Flexible and adaptable in a fast-paced industry
* Fluent in English (Swedish not necessary)



  

  





**WHO WE ARE**




 H&M Group is a collective of fashion brands and creative ventures redefining the industry from the inside out. We believe great design should be accessible, responsible, and uncompromising—where style, price, and sustainability exist in perfect balance.
   

  





 Our ambition is bold: to democratize fashion for a global audience while continuously pushing boundaries. Every decision we make is rooted in our customer—how they live, move, and express themselves through style.
   

  





 Behind it all is a global community of passionate, forward-thinking talent. Together, we harness creativity, scale, and innovation to shape a more inclusive, conscious, and progressive future for fashion.
   

  





**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE**




 At H&M Group, creativity thrives in a culture that is open, collaborative, and constantly evolving. You’ll be part of an environment where ideas are valued, individuality is celebrated, and growth is part of the everyday.
   

  





 With global opportunities and continuous development, you’ll be empowered to expand your perspective, refine your craft, and make a real impact—both within the company and across the wider fashion landscape.
   

  





**JOIN US**




 What sets us apart is not just what we create—but how we create it. Our culture, our values, and our shared ambition drive us forward—but it’s our people who define us.
   

  





 Bring your vision. Bring your energy. Bring your point of view.
   

  





 Take the next step in your career and be part of a journey where fashion is constantly being reimagined.

title: Expert Agentic Business Engineer
description:
**Job ID: 4460**
 We are seeking an experienced and highly hands-on Expert Agentic Business Engineer to design, build, and operationalize agentic AI solutions that solve real business problems in Asset & Wealth Management (AWM). You will work at the intersection of business transformation and advanced AI engineering – translating business needs into robust, well-governed agentic applications that can be safely deployed in a regulated banking environment.
   

  

**About Our Team**
 The AWM Chief Operating Office (COO) drives operational excellence, supports business transformation, and fosters innovation across Asset & Wealth Management. We focus on improving processes, leveraging technology, and ensuring strong governance and compliance – enabling sustainable growth and superior client experiences. The COO team collaborates across business, technology, and control functions to identify opportunities and deliver measurable impact.
   

  

 The AWM AI team is being established in AWM COO to accelerate delivery and scale of AI capabilities across AWM, with a strong focus on Private Banking and selected cross-area dependencies. We build common AI architecture and reusable patterns, support a disciplined governance model, and partner closely with business units, engineering teams, and control functions to turn an active AI pipeline into measurable outcomes.
   

  

**Main responsibilities in this role include, but are not limited to:**
* Own delivery from idea to production: frame business problems, shape agentic AI solution designs, prototype quickly, and industrialize into production-ready services with measurable outcomes.
* Build agentic AI applications: design and implement agents that can reason, plan, use tools, and interact with enterprise systems (e.g., advisor tooling, analytics platforms, internal APIs).
* Partner with stakeholders end-to-end: collaborate with Private Banking/AWM business leaders, architects, engineers, and control functions to ensure solutions deliver real value and can be adopted at scale.
* Engineer for governance and safety: implement evaluation strategies, guardrails, and human-in-the-loop patterns suitable for regulated banking environments (privacy, auditability, model risk expectations).
* Enable reuse and scale: build reusable patterns, components, and reference implementations to accelerate delivery across a portfolio of use cases.
* Operate and improve: monitor agent behavior in production, iterate on prompts/tools/logic based on evals and feedback, and continuously improve quality, cost, latency, and safety.


**Who You Are**
**This is the right role for you if you:**
* Are a curious, innovative team player with a can-do attitude, always looking for ways on how we can unlock the next level for our customers.


**Your background and skills include:**
* Hands-on agentic AI experience, including designing and building agent workflows (planner–executor, tool calling, multi-agent, human-in-the-loop).
* Strong LLM ops and evaluation mindset: quality/correctness, latency, cost, and safety; ability to define test sets and run disciplined experimentation.
* Guardrails and Responsible AI experience: input/output validation, prompt safety, data leakage prevention, and controls appropriate for regulated environments.
* Strong software engineering skills with Python (modular design, testing, version control) and comfort working in notebook-based experimentation.
* Cloud and integration experience: deploying AI services in AWS, integrating via REST APIs, and familiarity with containerisation/Kubernetes and CI/CD.
* Data/analytics platform experience (e.g., Snowflake) for grounding, retrieval, and context management.
* Business engineering strength: ability to translate stakeholder needs into solution requirements, KPIs, and adoption plans; strong communication and collaboration skills.


**Why join?**
 You will join a team building practical, high-impact AI capabilities across AWM – working on real advisor and operational workflows, with strong visibility and a clear focus on measurable outcomes. You will help shape how we build, govern, and scale agentic AI in a Nordic, regulated banking context. If you are passionate about agentic AI and eager to make a tangible impact in AWM, we invite you to apply and join our innovative COO team.
   

  

**What We Offer**
**People** 
 come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.
   

  

**A culture** 
 that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
   

  

**Hybrid working model –** 
 we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusion** 
 are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps**
 Submit your application no later than 24/05/2026. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Thomas Vedel Kristensen at thomas.v.kristensen@nordea.com.
   

  

**We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.**
**We build relationships.** 
 We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.
   

  

**We learn and develop.** 
 We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.
   

  

**We lead change.** 
 We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.
   

  

**We are Nordea.** 
 We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
   

  

**Only for candidates in Sweden:** 
 For union information, please contact finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACONordea@nordea.com.

title: Apex Programmatic Specialist
description:
**Company Description**
*Publicis Groupe is one of the largest communications groups in the world. We are building the agency model of the future and take a holistic approach to our clients’ challenges. By fusing together our media, creative, data and technology expertise, we are able to provide clients with integrated solutions, transformative ideas and compelling consumer experiences. We call it the Power of One.
   

  

 We are proud winner of many awards and nominations, both in Sweden and internationally. But nothing makes us prouder than to work with clients and brands such as P&G, Disney, H&M, Mondelez, Volvo, VISA, Lantmännen, LEGO, Spotify, Perrigo, Renault and many more.
   

  

 At Publicis, we put our people first. We are proud to be certified by Great Place to Work (2022, 2023, 2024, 2025) and have won the Diversity Charter Sweden Awards in the category Mental Health (2025). We have also been recognized with a Magnet Award for our engaging culture (2023). Our goal is to become the leading workplace in our industry. We are based in a fantastic office in Odenplan and offer a flexible work policy.*
**Overview**
**About APEX**
 APEX is the value-based solutions division of Publicis Media. They are a distinct and next- generation global trading entity which identifies new ways to deliver value to clients. Publicis innovative offerings via direct partnerships, principal investments and trade allow clients of all to have custom solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes.
   

  

**The role**
 We are now looking for a Programmatic Specialist to join our APEX team in Stockholm. In this role, you will be a key part of our programmatic delivery capability — working closely with the APEX Programmatic Product Owner and cross‑functional specialists to ensure that APEX programmatic activations are executed with precision, transparency, and measurable impact.
   

  

**Why join APEX?**
 APEX is in a strong growth phase, and you’ll be joining a team where curiosity, collaboration, and initiative are highly valued. We believe in sharing insights, challenging ways of working, and continuously improving — together.
   

  

 You’ll have the opportunity to play a key role in shaping and scaling APEX, work closely with leading digital specialists and partners, influence how we develop products, processes, and ways of working, and grow your expertise in a high-impact role within Publicis Media.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 As an APEX Programmatic Specialist, your responsibilities span the full programmatic delivery cycle. You will:
   

  

* Activate and manage APEX programmatic campaigns end‑to‑end, from setup and QA to go‑live and ongoing delivery.
* Own hands‑on DSP execution, including bidding, targeting, audience/data segmentation, optimization and in‑flight adjustments aligned to agreed KPIs.
* Monitor pacing, performance and profitability, proactively identifying risks and opportunities and recommending optimizations together with the APEX team and agency stakeholders.
* Support mid‑ and end‑of‑campaign reporting, contributing analysis, learnings and clear recommendations for future activations.
* Ensure strong operational discipline, keeping campaign status, documentation and actuals/spend up to date.
* Collaborate closely with agency teams, supporting delivery, transparency and insights as APEX operates as an internal vendor.


**Qualifications**
 We believe you will thrive in this role if you have:
   

  

* Academic or relevant post secondary education in marketing, media, or a related field.
* Experience working with digital campaign activation within programmatic.
* A strong interest in ad tech and digital platforms, with solid numerical confidence.
* The ability to move between tactical execution and strategic thinking.
* Experience analyzing campaign results and translating delivery into clear insights and actionable recommendations.
* Strong communication skills and confidence collaborating with multiple stakeholders.
* A proactive mindset, with a desire to improve, scale, and refine ways of working.
* A structured and independent working style, with strong ownership and the ability to meet deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
* A solution‑oriented mindset, high attention to detail, and strong analytical ambition.
* Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken.


**Additional Information**
 In addition to a welcoming culture and inclusive environment with passionate colleagues we offer you:
   

  

**Career Opportunities**
 Local and Global opportunities through the Power Of One.
   

  

**Personal and Professional Learning**
 Global AI learning platform where you can participate in +30 000 classes and connect with colleagues from all over the world.
   

  

**Flexible Ways of Working**
 We want to be an employer where work life balance is cherished. We love to spend time with our colleagues at the office but also believe in the output of your work not where the work is being done.
   

  

**Work Your World**
 For six weeks a year, the world is yours. Every employee can work anywhere on the planet where there's a Publicis office. A new city, a favorite locale, or even a different country - Go. Grow. Explore.
   

  

**Mental Health**
 The happiness and health of our people is paramount to us. We work continuously on maintaining a healthy and safe working environment through initiatives that facilitate the well-being of our employees.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Generous benefit package.
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: Senior Public Sector Account Executive, AWS Public Sector Sweden
description:
**Description**
 Do you thrive where leadership meets growth ambition and world-class technology? AWS Sweden is hiring a Senior Account Manager to lead transformative deals and redefine how Public Sector Sweden innovates with cloud and Agentic AI. Bring your passion for building businesses, your operational excellence, and your deep industry insight and help our customers accelerate their digital transformations at scale.
   

  

 As Senior Account Manager, you will manage and grow strategic Public Sector accounts in Sweden. You will own the go-to-market strategy and execution for strategic accounts, drive revenue growth, and set the vision for how AWS partners with public sector organizations to reshape the industry. You will report to the Swedish Leadership and collaborate closely with regional stakeholders to deliver on bold growth ambitions. This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden with travel required to key customer sites.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Build and maintain trusted senior relationships with strategic public sector accounts. Foster partnerships anchored on customer obsession, accountability, and continuous improvement.
* Partner with cross-functional teams (Business Development, Solutions Architecture, Partner Organization, Marketing, Customer Success) to solve customers' most complex business and technical challenges.
* Identify transformational opportunities, de-risk execution, and replicate success through scalable plays and mechanisms.
* Use data and metrics to manage pipeline health, forecasting accuracy, and sales productivity.
* Represent AWS at industry events and evangelize our innovation across Public Sector
* Accelerate customer adoption & develop long-term strategic relationships with key accounts
* Travel as necessary


**About The Team**
 Diverse Experiences
   

  

 Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

 Why AWS
   

  

 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
   

  

 Work/Life Balance
   

  

 We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
   

  

 Inclusive Team Culture
   

  

 Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.
   

  

 Mentorship and Career Growth
   

  

 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* - Sales Experience in management of large, complex public sector accounts or equivalent
* - Experience engaging and influencing senior executives, demonstrating a strong familiarity with decision-making processes in public sector customers
* - Solving problems with technology in the Public Sector industries experience
* - Experience with pipeline management skills to include utilization of Salesforce or other CRM tools
* - Bachelor's degree in a relevant field or equivalent work experience
* - Swedish-language communication skills, both written and verbal
* - Deep understanding of cloud, infrastructure, data, analytics, and AI/ML with ability to translate technology into strategic business outcomes


**Preferred Qualifications**
* - Experience influencing innovation through a partner ecosystem with either solutions or programs that have demonstrated success at public sector customers
* - Experience delivering results for large, cross-functional initiatives/projects
* - Experience working with global cross-functional teams
* - Established network in Public Sector within Sweden


 Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
   

  

 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 - AWS EMEA SARL (Sweden Branch)
   

  

 Job ID: A10408343

title: Digital Merchandising Lead - Vitamin Well Group
description:
*At* 
***Vitamin Well Group,***
*we provide great-tasting drinks, snacks, and protein products. Our brands Vitamin Well, NOCCO, and Barebells are available across most of Europe, North America, the MENA region, and parts of Asia Pacific. With a revenue of 650 million euros, we operate in over 40 markets, with headquarters in Stockholm and offices in fourteen countries around the world.* 




  





 Our Digital Sales team operate across markets on a global scope. Together, they drive and scale our D2C and Marketplace business, combining commercial focus with data-driven execution. Digital is a key part of our future growth strategy, and the team plays a central role in accelerating sales, strengthening our direct consumer relationships, and shaping the next phase of our digital journey.
 



  





**About the role** 




 We are looking for a
 **Digital Merchandising Lead** 
 to drive conversion and commercial impact across our D2C channels.
 



  





 In this role, you will lead our digital merchandising and on-site commercial strategy – with a clear focus on conversion, AOV and profitable growth. You will shape how our product assortment is presented, activated and optimized based on on-site behavior and commercial opportunities.
 



 The role combines strategy and execution – you set the direction while staying close to the work across site merchandising, campaigns and conversion optimization.
 



 You will not have direct reports initially, but you will work closely with our Digital Merchandiser and local markets.
 



  





**Key responsibilities** 



* Own both strategy and hands-on execution within digital merchandising, with a strong focus on on-site optimization and commercial impact
* Own how products, categories and campaigns are presented on-site to maximize engagement, conversion and revenue
* Use on-site behavior, trends and customer data to continuously optimize assortment exposure and commercial priorities
* Plan and execute commercial activations such as campaigns, seasonal pushes and product launches
* Drive growth through commercial levers such as cross-sell, upsell, bundling and promotions
* Analyze performance and customer behavior to generate insights and continuously improve key KPIs such as CVR, AOV and RPV
* Define and drive an experimentation roadmap (A/B testing) to validate and scale initiatives
* Collaborate closely with CRM, performance marketing and brand teams to ensure a consistent and high-performing customer experience
* Act as a go-to person for merchandising, contributing to stronger commercial thinking and best practices across teams



  





**Who you are** 




 You have 5–10+ years of experience within e-commerce, digital merchandising or CRO, and are confident optimizing on-site performance across product exposure, campaigns and customer journeys.
 



 You have a strong commercial instinct and understand how to combine data, creativity and customer behavior to unlock growth.
 



 You are both analytical and creative – comfortable using data to identify opportunities, while also translating insights into impactful on-site experiences, campaigns and product storytelling that drive conversion and revenue.
 



 You thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy working close to the business, continuously optimizing the on-site experience to drive conversion and sales.
 



  





**What you will bring** 



* 5–10+ years of experience within e-commerce, digital merchandising or CRO, preferably within D2C
* Hands-on experience working with e-commerce platforms (e.g. Shopify or similar), as well as merchandising, personalization and CRO/testing tools
* Experience using AI within merchandising and content workflows (e.g. personalization, automation or generative AI); familiarity with emerging AI commerce trends is a plus
* Strong understanding of on-site customer behavior and how to translate insights into commercial actions that drive conversion and growth
* Proven ability to optimize key commercial levers such as product exposure, cross-sell, upsell, bundling and promotions
* Experience with A/B testing and experimentation, combined with strong analytical skills and experience using tools such as Google Analytics or similar
* A creative and commercial mindset, with the ability to turn insights into engaging on-site experiences
* You communicate clearly in both Swedish and English, written and spoken



  





**Welcome with your application at the latest May 8th 2026.** 




*We will review applications on an ongoing basis and the position may be filled before the last application day. We appreciate LinkedIn, but to get a full picture of your experience, we ask you to also include a CV.* 




  





**Life at Vitamin Well Group** 




 Our growing family is made up of over 600 employees, representing a variety of cultures, experiences and diverse backgrounds. This diversity is a great asset as we continue our expansion and evolve our portfolio. At the same time, Vitamin Well is still very much a Swedish company, promoting Swedish values when it comes to both business and people. We treat each other in a fair and equal way, and we respect one another based on our qualities. As we expand Vitamin Well Group’s business, we are also committed to minimizing our environmental impact and continuously strive to lower emissions from for example production, logistics, and packaging.
 



  





 Working at Vitamin Well, we provide a dynamic environment defined by ambition, dedication, and an entrepreneurial spirit. In our action-oriented culture, the ability to make swift decisions is paramount in bringing ideas to fruition. We are committed to fostering an atmosphere where every individual's unique strengths contribute to our collective success. As a health and exercise-oriented company, we inspire an active lifestyle by partnering with top-performing athletes, but we also promote and support exercise among our employees.
 



  





*As part of the recruitment process, we may ask you to conduct a personality test as well as a test that measures logical ability. The purpose of the tests is to evaluate candidates as objective as possible and identify the best possible match for the role. The tests are based on solid research and are taken through our partner Alva Labs.*

title: Senior/Staff Data Platform Engineer - (Bangkok based, relocation provided)
description:
**About Agoda**
 At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.
   

  

 Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.
   

  

 No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.
   

  

**Position Overview**
 The Data Platform Engineer is responsible for designing, building, and operating a cloud-native data platform built on Snowflake and public cloud infrastructure. This role owns the full lifecycle of the data platform — from cloud environment provisioning and data lake architecture to transformation pipelines, security enforcement, and data sharing — enabling reliable, governed, and scalable data access across the organization.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Provision and manage cloud environments including storage, networking, and IAM across public cloud platforms (AWS / GCP / Azure).
* Architect and administer Snowflake as the organization's unified Data Lake and Data Warehouse platform.
* Define and enforce Data Access Policies with encryption, IAM, and Snowflake network policies.
* Build and maintain dbt transformation models with Git-based version control across staging and production data layers.
* Define platform standards, and best practices for data ingestion, transformation, and consumption layers.


**Required Skills & Experience**
* Hands-on experience with at least one major public cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or Azure), covering storage, networking, and IAM.
* Strong Snowflake administration skills including RBAC design, Data Sharing, external tables, and Snowpark.
* Proficiency in dbt (Core or Cloud) for model development, testing, and documentation.
* Experience with Infrastructure-as-Code tooling such as Terraform for cloud resource provisioning.
* Experience with pipeline orchestration using Snowflake Scheduling, Apache Airflow, or equivalent tools.


**Scope of Ownership**
* Responsible for the end-to-end design, provisioning, and operation of the cloud data platform — spanning raw ingestion zones through to curated, analytics-ready data layers — ensuring reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency across all environments.
* Maintains overall responsibility for the unified data warehouse and lake platform architecture, including performance tuning, resource management, cost governance, and the evolution of the platform as organizational data needs grow.
* Owns the transformation layer from design through to production, ensuring data models, testing frameworks, and documentation are accurate, consistent, and fit for consumption across all downstream use cases.
* Drives the definition and adoption of platform-wide standards covering data ingestion, transformation, and consumption patterns, and evaluates tooling and architectural decisions as the platform matures.
