title: SAP VIM Specialist
description:
**Hiring: SAP VIM specialist**
 We’re looking for a
 **SAP VIM specialist** 
 join us at Telia.
   

  

 I am Eva Tallat-Kelpšienė I am leading a Sourcing and Supply Chain Solutions team at Telia group. I am looking to bring in another SAP VIM specialist to help us out with all the exciting work we do!
   

  

 This role is responsible for supporting day-to-day operations and delivering minor enhancements within SAP Vendor Invoice Management (VIM) primary in SAP s/4 environments, supporting finance users and stakeholders across the Nordic countries (Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland).
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
* Provide day-to-day operational support for SAP VIM, including incident management (minor to critical), troubleshooting, service requests, and root cause analysis in line with OLAs.
* Monitor invoice processing flows, queues, and background jobs to ensure stable and timely end-to-end processing.
* Deliver minor enhancements and configuration changes in collaboration with Finance and related SAP teams (FI/MM, Integration, Basis).
* Support testing (UT, SIT, UAT), go-live, and hyper care activities for VIM changes.
* Maintain documentation, operational procedures, and contribute to continuous improvement of automation, efficiency, and system performance.
* Work in alignment with ITIL, SAP change management standards, and agile frameworks (e.g., SAFe), using tools such as ServiceNow and JIRA.


**What will help you thrive in this role:**
* Bachelor’s degree in IT, Business, Finance, or related field (or equivalent experience).
* 3+ years of hands-on SAP VIM experience, preferably in SAP S/4HANA environments.
* Solid understanding of invoice-to-pay (P2P/I2P) processes and finance operations.
* Experience supporting SAP VIM operations, including incident management, configuration, workflow, and exception handling.
* Collaboration experience with SAP FI/MM and technical/integration teams; familiarity with transport and change management processes.
* Knowledge of ITIL-based support models and experience with tools such as JIRA or ServiceNow.
* Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills; fluent in English.
* Willingness to support critical incidents outside regular business hours when required.


**Join us to shape smarter living for people, businesses, and the planet**
 At Telia, we’re a team of 15 000 colleagues across the Nordics and Baltics, passionate about technology and connectivity. As a
 **Leading Telecommunications Operator** 
 &
 **Climate Leader** 
 , we drive change with large-scale, impactful projects while building a sustainable, inclusive world.
   

  

 Here, you can
 **grow your skills, lead meaningful projects, and thrive in a supportive, diverse environment.** 
 Whether you’re taking your first step or your next, Telia is a place to grow, belong, and make a real impact.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.
   

  

**Ready to make better happen?**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **May** 
**19, 2026.** 
 Please note that we might close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply!
   

  

 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner.** 
 Background and reference checks will be part of the process.
   

  

**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by
 **professional skills and growth potential.** 
 We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where
 **your uniqueness is our strength.**
**Join us and let’s make better happen - together!**

title: Senior Producer [Fixed Term]
description:
As a Producer you will work with various teams and disciplines, ensuring the roadmap and dependencies properly line up. Focusing on project management tasks, you will craft plans, put them in motion, and facilitate achievement of our goals. Working closely with a Product owner, the producer facilitates the team, motivating and removing blockers from them whilst constantly driving the project towards success.
   

  

 In this role, Avalanche Studios is searching for an adaptive, curious and enthusiastic leader with game development experience who can inspire, drive and energize their team.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Reporting to the Production Director, support the Product owner by being responsible for planning setup, team progress, key deliverables and project milestones to ensure timely delivery.
* Facilitate team ceremonies connected to production ways of working (scrum and/or Waterfall management).
* Help the team by removing blockers, improving communication, collaboration and creating a high level of ownership.
* Build efficient workflows using tools like Jira to optimize development processes.


**Who You Are**
* At least 10 years experience as a Producer.
* Been involved with at least one game project through a complete development cycle.
* Experienced in AAA production.
* Has directly led a full or significant part of a multi-disciplinary development team.
* Extensive knowledge in game, Agile and software development methods.
* Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills in English.
* Collaborative spirit.


 Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.
   

  

**The Stockholm location**
 Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.
   

  

**Our values**
 At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
   

  

 At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.

title: Channel Account Executive (CAE) - Partner Resale Programme (Nordics)
description:
**Your work days are brighter here.**
 We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.
   

  

**About The Team**
 Workmates pride themselves on winning while having fun! That means supporting each other while driving accountability for amazing results and performance. This team is no different. Everything we do inspires a brighter work day for all. The Enterprise Sales team at Workday helps the company to continue to grow by balancing integrity and innovation, ensuring Workmates have the environment to bring their best self, and get better by pushing and developing themselves and the Workmates around them.
   

  

**About The Role**
 At Workday, we put people at the center of everything we do. For the past decade, we’ve built a world-class direct sales engine, earning a 95% customer satisfaction rating along the way. Now we’re entering a new chapter—and you can be at the heart of it.
   

  

 We’re looking for a Channel Account Executive (CAE) to help scale our impact in the small and midsize market through a high-performing ecosystem of reseller partners. This is a player–manager, quota-carrying role for someone who loves building something new, thrives in collaboration, and knows how to win through others.
   

  

 As a Channel AE, you’re more than a partner manager—you’re a trusted advisor, a coach, and a co-seller who ensures every customer feels the “Workday way.”
   

  

****Key Responsibilities****
* Opportunity Management & Co‑Selling


 You’ll combine hands-on selling with partner enablement to accelerate growth.
   

  

* Player–Manager Execution You will work side-by-side with partners on strategic opportunities, bringing your Workday product and industry expertise to help shape deal strategy, navigate complexity, and close high‑value opportunities.
* Coaching & Enablement You’ll spend as much time empowering as executing—training partner sellers on product positioning, competitive differentiation, and the Workday GO methodology so they can confidently lead customers through the journey.
* Integrated Forecasting You’ll be fully embedded in the regional sales team, owning a consistent and disciplined forecast for all partner‑led business. You’ll operate with the same rigor and standards as our direct Account Executives, ensuring visibility and predictability of the partner pipeline.
* Territory & Ecosystem Strategy


 You’ll help shape how Workday shows up in your territory through and with partners.
   

  

* Holistic Territory Management You’ll manage a territory where both new business and customer expansion are intentionally driven through the partner ecosystem—rewarding those who build strong, sustainable partner capability and trusted customer relationships.
* Market Expansion You’ll identify and onboard new partners where we see geographic or industry whitespace. As an early leader in the Partner Resale Programme, you’ll help open new markets and prove the power of the resale model.
* Culture & Relationship Advocacy


 You’ll protect what makes Workday special—our culture, our quality, and our customer experience.
   

  

* Preserving the DNA You’ll ensure partners deliver the same high‑quality, customer‑first experience that has defined Workday for years. You’ll advocate for best practices that keep our customers at the center of every decision.
* Strategic Alignment You’ll act as the connective tissue between partner‑led motions and our direct sales teams, helping to eliminate silos and ensure a consistent, seamless journey for customers across all touchpoints.
* Executive Influence You’ll build trusted relationships with partner leadership and serve as the internal “voice of the partner.” You’ll bring partner and customer insights back to Workday to help shape our Product, Marketing, and go‑to‑market strategies.


**About You**
****Basic Qualifications****
* Experience: 5+ years of success in enterprise sales, with a specific focus on—or a strong pivot toward—channel sales and partner ecosystems.
* Sales Instinct: A verifiable track record of meeting multi-million dollar quotas, with the maturity to hit those numbers through the performance of others.
* Coaching Mindset: You are an expert communicator who finds satisfaction in transferring knowledge and building a network of confident, credible partner sellers.
* Communication: Excellent verbal and written language skills in Swedish and English.


**Additional Qualifications**
* Pioneer’s Mindset: Comfortable with the ambiguity of a growing programme; you enjoy "shaping" a role rather than inheriting a "paint-by-numbers" territory.
* Matrix Navigation: Ability to operate within regional sales teams, influencing stakeholders across a highly matrixed global organization.
* Quality Obsession: An instinct to protect the customer experience, ensuring that partner-led deals never compromise on the satisfaction ratings Workday is known for.
* Willingness to Travel: Ability to travel up to [75%] within the assigned territory to be on-site with partners and customers.


**Our Offer**
* Competitive remuneration, restricted stock units & an ESPP
* Health insurance for employees and their families, pension plan & more
* Flexible working hours and work from home option
* A powerful product, named as a leader by Gartner for Cloud HCM Suites, Cloud Core Financial Management Suites and Cloud Financial Planning and Analysis
* Structured employee onboarding & Workday sales academy to ensure a quick and successful ramp up
* Team events, employee assistance program, perks/discounts offerings, free fresh fruit & snacks in our office locations and mor


**Our Approach to Flexible Work**
 With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply
 **spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field** 
 with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.
   

  

 At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.
   

  

**Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!**
 At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security. Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers.
   

  

 Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.
   

  

 In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.
   

  

 ,

title: Senior DevOps / Platform Engineer
description:
**Job Title:** Senior DevOps / Platform Engineer (EU – Remote)**Location:** Fully remote within the European Union. Candidates must reside in, and be authorized to work in, an EU member state that participates in the Schengen Area.
Do you love building the cloud platforms that power mission-critical software, and do you want to know that your work helps keep communities safer? Kaseware builds case management and investigations software trusted by law enforcement and corporate security teams around the world. Our European customers depend on a platform that is reliable, secure, and engineered to meet the demands of serving the public – and that is where you come in.


We are looking for a Senior DevOps / Platform Engineer to join our Architecture Team. You will design, build, automate, and operate the cloud infrastructure platform that powers Kaseware’s commercial and international customer deployments. The role combines deep Kubernetes and Linux expertise with strong infrastructure-as-code, GitOps, and observability discipline. You will work across our cloud footprint – primarily Azure and AWS – partner closely with engineering and customer operations teams, and have a direct hand in shaping how Kaseware delivers and runs software at scale.

**Responsibilities and Duties:**

* Design, build, and operate cloud infrastructure across Azure and AWS commercial environments supporting Kaseware’s customer deployments
* Administer and operate Kubernetes clusters (AKS, EKS), including upgrades, networking, RBAC, storage, and platform-level tooling
* Author, modify, and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform – including modules, state management, and multi-environment deployment patterns
* Implement and operate GitOps continuous delivery using ArgoCD; build and maintain workflows with Argo Workflows where applicable
* Author and maintain Helm charts – templating, dependencies, and release management
* Build, maintain, and enhance CI/CD pipelines and automation across the development and deployment lifecycle
* Instrument, monitor, and troubleshoot cloud and Kubernetes workloads using Datadog and other observability tooling (Prometheus, Grafana, Elastic stack, Fluentd, Istio, etc.)
* Support customer installations and consult on deployment topology for both cloud and on-premises environments
* Administer development environments and developer tooling that keep engineering teams productive
* Participate in production incident response and on-call rotations across distributed time zones, supporting any Kaseware system other than U.S. federal government protected environments
* Contribute new ideas, tools, and patterns to the Kaseware technical stack – we encourage every team member to innovate

**Required Education:**


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.

**Required Skills & Experience:**

* 4+ years of professional experience in DevOps, Platform Engineering, Site Reliability Engineering, or Cloud Infrastructure roles
* Strong Kubernetes platform expertise, including cluster lifecycle management, networking, RBAC, CSI / storage classes and provisioners, and ingress controllers (NGINX or comparable); fluent with kubectl
* Hands-on Terraform experience – able to write new modules, modify existing ones, manage state, and operate multi-environment workflows
* Production experience with ArgoCD and GitOps deployment patterns; familiarity with Argo Workflows is a plus
* Helm chart authoring – templating, dependencies, releases, and CLI usage
* Container technologies (Docker, BuildKit, container registries) including image authoring, local testing, and registry management
* Comprehensive Linux administration and command-line proficiency
* Cloud platform experience with Azure and AWS commercial environments; AKS and EKS in particular
* Cloud database and data services, especially PostgreSQL; experience with NoSQL stores such as ArangoDB or MongoDB is a plus
* Observability and monitoring – production experience with Datadog and/or comparable tooling such as Prometheus, Grafana, Elasticsearch, Fluentd, and Istio
* Networking concepts, technologies, and troubleshooting (DNS, TLS, routing, load balancing)
* CI/CD pipeline design and implementation experience (GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, or comparable platforms); strong understanding of software development lifecycle and workflows
* Comprehensive understanding of Git repositories, branching strategies, and the Git CLI
* Scripting and/or programming proficiency in Bash and Python
* Demonstrated ability to manage and automate deployments for complex applications across cloud and on-premises environments
* Single Sign-On and Identity Provider experience, especially Keycloak (preferred)
* Strong written and verbal communication; able to collaborate effectively across distributed teams and time zones
* Ability to work independently, prioritize against team objectives, and operate effectively in an asynchronous remote environment
* Other duties as needed.

**Other Requirements:**

* Language: English (business fluency required for technical communication, documentation, and customer-facing collaboration)
* Residence and work authorization: Candidate must reside in, and have the legal right to work in, an EU member state that participates in the Schengen Area
* Availability: Willingness to participate in on-call and incident response rotations across global time zones for any Kaseware system

**Benefits:**

* Base salary range: €80,000 - €100,000 per year
* Competitive salary in an entrepreneurial environment
* Total compensation and benefits package will be administered through Kaseware’s Employer of Record (EOR) partner and aligned with the local market in the candidate’s country of residence

**About Kaseware:**


Kaseware is a rapidly growing start-up located in the Denver metro area. We build state-of-the-art software for law enforcement and corporate security customers. We serve those that serve our communities and make our world safer.

  

Due to the nature of our business, you must be able to pass a full CJIS compliant fingerprint based background check, which is required for individuals needing access to criminal justice information (CJI).  



**We expect this role to be open until June 30th, 2026 or until filled.**

title: System & Application Manager Analysis Applications
description:
Job Description
  
The client is seeking an experienced System and Application Manager to join the IT division responsible for power system analysis. This function supports critical systems that enable operational planning, analytical capabilities, and long-term development of the power infrastructure.
  
The selected candidate will become part of a rapidly expanding and business-critical team, contributing to the management of both internally developed and third-party applications within a complex IT landscape. The role also supports ongoing transformation initiatives focused on DevOps practices and agile methodologies.
  
You will be part of a newly established team responsible for maintaining purchased systems and supporting delivery teams working on in-house applications.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Oversee and manage both in-house and third-party applications within the operational planning domain
  
Act as a key liaison between delivery teams, IT operations, and other technical stakeholders
  
Translate business requirements into scalable and efficient IT solutions
  
Contribute to system architecture, technical requirement definition, and lifecycle management
  
Drive and support strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing system efficiency and performance
  
Lead or assist in change initiatives and smaller technical assignments within the domain
  
Coordinate closely with DevOps specialists, infrastructure teams, IT security, and business units
  
Ensure system availability, stability, and compliance with ITIL processes (incident, problem, and change management)
  
Plan and execute system upgrades, releases, and testing activities
  
Manage system documentation, operational procedures, and access control structures
  
Ensure adherence to security standards and regulatory requirements
  
Serve as the primary point of contact for vendors and external partners
  
Promote knowledge sharing across teams and support technical collaboration
  
Assist in automation initiatives where applicable
  
  

Technical Environment
  
**Development Technologies:** C++, C#, Python, React
  
**Platforms:** Linux, Windows, Kubernetes
  
**CI/CD Tools:** Azure DevOps (future migration to GitLab), Argo CD, Artifactory, JFrog Xray, SonarQube, Git
  
**Monitoring & Observability:** OP5, Prometheus, Loki, Grafana
  
**Automation Tools:** Ansible, Terraform, Helm
  
**Infrastructure:** Primarily on-premises environments due to high security requirements
  
  

Required Experience
  
Proven experience as a System and Application Manager or similar role
  
Strong understanding of IT operations, system lifecycle management, and DevOps practices
  
Experience working in agile environments
  
Exposure to IT security standards and compliance requirements
  
Ability to coordinate across multiple teams and stakeholders
  
  

Required Skills
  
Azure DevOps
  
Python & scripting (e.g., PowerShell)
  
SQL databases
  
Monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, OP5)
  
Jira
  
Windows Server
  
Agile methodologies
  
IT security practices
  
  

Education Requirement
  
Minimum of 120 credits / 180 ECTS in IT or related field
  
  

Languages
  
English – Proficient
  
Swedish – Proficient
  
  

Job Mode
  
Hybrid (Up to 50% remote)
  
  

**Application Method:** Interested candidates can apply by sending their profile to hr@semiconservicenordic.com

title: Kubernetes Platform Engineer
description:
Conoa hjälper företag att modernisera IT-infrastruktur med fokus på Kubernetes, Cloud Native, Container-teknologi, Hybrid Cloud, IaC, CI/CD, Automatisering och DevOps.


Linux och Open Source finns i vårt DNA, och vi utvecklas ständigt genom att dela kunskap med varandra. Vi satsar stenhårt på kompetensutveckling genom en generös utbildningsbudget, och våra medarbetare belönas för att hålla sina kunskaper i absolut framkant.

**Om rollen**


Är du en Kubernetes-expert med en passion för att lösa komplexa problem och hjälpa kunder att bygga skalbara och pålitliga plattformar? Har du erfarenhet av både cloud- och on-prem? Om så är fallet, tror vi att du skulle passa bra i rollen som Kubernetes konsult på Conoa!


Som Kuberneteskonsult på Conoa kommer du att arbeta nära våra kunder för att förstå deras affärsbehov och designa anpassade lösningar baserade på Kubernetes som är skalbara, pålitliga och säkra både i cloud- och on-prem miljöer.


Du kommer dels att vara ansvarig för att utvärdera våra kunders befintliga infrastruktur, identifiera områden som kan förbättras och implementera bästa praxis för Kubernetes-distribution, hantering och optimering. Men du kommer även att kunna komma in i tidiga faser av projekt och genomföra såväl design, arkitektur och implementation. Vi rekryterar både till vårt kontor i Stockholm och i Göteborg.

**Dina ansvarsområden**

* Arbeta nära kunder för att förstå deras affärsbehov och designa anpassade lösningar baserade på Kubernetes.
* Utvärdera kundernas befintliga infrastruktur, identifiera områden som kan förbättras och implementera bästa praxis för Kubernetes-distribution, hantering och optimering.
* Tillhandahålla teknisk vägledning och stöd under hela projektets livscykel, från design till distribution och underhåll.
* Samarbeta med våra interna team för att säkerställa att våra lösningar är anpassade till våra kunders behov och mål.
* Hålla dig uppdaterad inom de senaste trenderna och utvecklingarna inom Kubernetes och cloudinfrastruktur.

**Kvalifikationer**

* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av att bygga och hantera plattformar baserade på Kubernetes.
* Stark förståelse för containeriseringsteknologier som Docker och Podman.
* Lång erfarenhet av Linux.
* Erfarenhet av automatiseringsverktyg som Ansible och Terraform.
* Kännedom om cloudplattformar som AWS, Azure eller GCP, samt on-prem infrastruktur.
* Kännedom om Kubernetes-plattformar som Rancher, OpenShift och Tanzu.
* Erfarenhet av övervaknings- och loggningsverktyg som Prometheus, Grafana och ELK-stacken.
* God problemlösningsförmåga och noggrannhet.
* Stark kommunikations- och samarbetsförmåga.
* Erfarenhet av arbete som konsult eller i en kundnära roll är ett plus.
* Du talar och skriver svenska och engelska.

**Vad vi erbjuder**


Om du är redo för en spännande utmaning inom och vill arbeta i ett av Sveriges ledande konsultbolag inom Kubernetes så har vi mycket att erbjuda. Här får du arbeta i teknikens framkant med utmaningar och projekt i moderna IT-miljöer där skalbarhet, säkerhet och automatisering är prioriterat. Du är en del av ett fantastiskt team med en varm och familjär kultur.


På Conoa finns ett erfaret team med säljare och konsultchefer, som genom moderna arbetssätt och rätt nätverk hittar det perfekta uppdraget för dig.


Du kommer att arbeta i uppdrag där du utmanas och får använda din kompetens på bästa sätt. För den som vill kan detta kombineras med kortare specialistuppdrag, utbildning och workshops.

**Om Conoa**


Conoa grundades 2012 med en stark övertygelse om att Linux och Open Source-baserade lösningar är grundbultar i modern infrastruktur. Vårt mål var att bygga ett starkt teknikfokuserat företag med en företagskultur som främjar kompetensutveckling för att ständigt vara relevanta och hjälpa företag in i nya IT-miljöer.


2015 inleddes vår satsning inom Container-teknologi då vi blev första partner i Europa till Docker. Resan fortsatte med viktiga roller i stora kundprojekt, och att vi som första bolag i Sverige utsågs till både Kubernetes Certified Service Provider (KCSP) och Kubernetes Training Partner (KTP). Under dessa år har vi växt fram som en av Sveriges främsta specialister inom Kubernetes, Cloud Native och Container-teknologi.


Vi har förmånen att ha ett 30-tal av Sveriges främsta specialister som medarbetare och vi hjälper några av Sveriges ledande företag att implementera modern Cloud Native infrastruktur.


Sedan april 2021 är vi en del av Proact. Genom samgåendet får vi ännu större möjlighet att utvecklas som specialister, samt att bredda våra tjänster och geografiska närvaro.

**Vi är på en spännande tillväxtresa!**

**Vill du vara med?**

**Hör av dig!**


Avdelning
Technology & Services
Roll
Kubernetes Platform Engineer
Platser
Göteborg, Stockholm
Distansarbete
Hybridarbete
Kontakt Kristina Lindquist Recruitment Manager

title: Platform Engineer - OpenShift
description:
Conoa hjälper företag att modernisera IT-infrastruktur med fokus på Kubernetes, Cloud Native, Container-teknologi, Hybrid Cloud, IaC, CI/CD, Automatisering och DevOps.


Linux och Open Source finns i vårt DNA, och vi utvecklas ständigt genom att dela kunskap med varandra. Vi satsar stenhårt på kompetensutveckling genom en generös utbildningsbudget, och våra medarbetare belönas för att hålla sina kunskaper i absolut framkant.

**Om rollen**


Är du en erfaren och tekniskt kunnig Platform Engineer med fokus på OpenShift? Vi söker en passionerad och kompetent person som vill jobba som expertkonsult inom den tekniska stacken för att bygga en robust OpenShift-plattform. Om du har 3-5 års erfarenhet inom området och vill vara en del av vårt Conoateam, kan du vara den person vi letar efter. Rekryteringen är till vårt Göteborgskontor.


**Dina ansvarsområden**

* Designa, implementera och underhålla en OpenShift-plattform som är skalbar, säker och högtillgänglig.
* Som konsult samarbeta med våra kunder för att förstå deras behov och anpassa plattformen efter deras specifika krav.
* Automatisera deployment, skalning och drift av applikationer på OpenShift-plattformen.
* Övervaka och felsöka plattformens prestanda och hantera incidenter vid behov.
* Hålla dig uppdaterad om de senaste trenderna och utvecklingarna inom OpenShift och containeriseringsområdet.

**Kvalifikationer**

* 3-5 års erfarenhet av att bygga och underhålla OpenShift-plattformar.
* Djup förståelse för containeriseringsteknologier som Docker och Podman.
* Erfarenhet av automatisering och orkestrering med verktyg som Ansible, Terraform och Kubernetes Operators.
* God kännedom om cloudplattformar som AWS, Azure eller Google Cloud.
* Erfarenhet av övervaknings- och loggningsverktyg som Prometheus, Grafana och ELK-stacken.
* Utmärkt problemlösningsförmåga och uppmärksamhet på detaljer.
* Starka kommunikations- och samarbetsförmågor.

**Vad vi erbjuder**


Om du är redo för en spännande utmaning inom OpenShift och vill arbeta i ett av Sveriges ledande konsultbolag inom Kubernetes så har vi mycket att erbjuda. Här får du arbeta i teknikens framkant med utmaningar och projekt i moderna IT-miljöer där skalbarhet, säkerhet och automatisering är prioriterat. Du är en del av ett fantastiskt team med en varm och familjär kultur.


På Conoa finns ett erfaret team med säljare och konsultchefer, som genom moderna arbetssätt och rätt nätverk hittar det perfekta uppdraget för dig.


Du kommer att arbeta i uppdrag där du utmanas och får använda din kompetens på bästa sätt. För den som vill kan detta kombineras med kortare specialistuppdrag, utbildning och workshops.


**Om Conoa**


Conoa grundades 2012 med en stark övertygelse om att Linux och Open Source-baserade lösningar är grundbultar i modern infrastruktur. Vårt mål var att bygga ett starkt teknikfokuserat företag med en företagskultur som främjar kompetensutveckling för att ständigt vara relevanta och hjälpa företag in i nya IT-miljöer.


2015 inleddes vår satsning inom Container-teknologi då vi blev första partner i Europa till Docker. Resan fortsatte med viktiga roller i stora kundprojekt, och att vi som första bolag i Sverige utsågs till både Kubernetes Certified Service Provider (KCSP) och Kubernetes Training Partner (KTP). Under dessa år har vi växt fram som en av Sveriges främsta specialister inom Kubernetes, cloud native och container-teknologi.


Vi har förmånen att ha ett 30-tal av Sveriges främsta specialister som medarbetare och vi hjälper några av Sveriges ledande företag att implementera modern Cloud Native infrastruktur.


Sedan april 2021 är vi en del av Proact. Genom samgåendet får vi ännu större möjlighet att utvecklas som specialister, samt att bredda våra tjänster och geografiska närvaro.

**Vi är på en spännande tillväxtresa!**

**Vill du vara med?**

**Hör av dig!**


Avdelning
Technology & Services
Roll
Platform Engineer - OpenShift
Platser
Göteborg, Stockholm
Distansarbete
Hybridarbete
Kontakt Kristina Lindquist Recruitment Manager
